title: Pentacalia vallecaucana
taxonomy: Pentacalia vallecaucana is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Pentacalia vallecaucana is also classified as a member of the tribe Senecioneae. Further classification within the genus Pentacalia places it as a species within the Pentacalia genus. The exact classification levels of Pentacalia vallecaucana are Pentacalia vallecaucana, Asteraceae family, Senecioneae tribe, and Pentacalia genus.

intro: Pentacalia vallecaucana is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a shrubby plant with medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Pentacalia vallecaucana contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory infections, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues.
